summary: Descriptive overview of defenseofothers in substantive law: alter ego rule, reasonable belief.
key_points: alter ego rule is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for defenseofothers are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine defenseofothers in litigation.
The doctrine of defenseofothers has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding defenseofothers requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
